county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1818 from Cape Girardeau, Ste. Genevieve. County seat: Fredericktown. Missouri Lead Mining District — lead mining from French colonial era to present.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Fredericktown holds land records from 1818. Madison County is in the St. Francois Mountains with significant lead mining heritage — Mine La Motte was one of the earliest lead mines in Missouri (French colonial era). Battle of Fredericktown (1861) was an early Civil War engagement. Madison County Historical Society has genealogical files. French colonial land grants in this area. Strong German and Irish miner immigration. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Check Cape Girardeau and Ste. Genevieve for pre-1818 records. St. Michael Catholic Church records from early settlement.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1818

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Deed Records -- Recorder of Deeds, Fredericktown
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation (1818)

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Probate Records -- Probate Court, Fredericktown
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, administrations, and guardianship records from 1818. Missouri probate courts also handle some adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Marriage Records -- Recorder of Deeds / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records from 1818. Missouri marriage records kept at county level by Recorder of Deeds. FamilySearch has indexed many Missouri marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1820-1950 for Madison. Missouri territorial census 1820; state census 1844, 1852, 1856, 1860, 1864, 1868, 1876 (incomplete). Slave schedules 1850, 1860.

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Original Land Entry Records -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Madison County. Missouri is a public land state — BLM GLO records document first transfer of land from federal government. Some areas have earlier French/Spanish colonial grants.

source_profiles.source_name: Civil War Records -- Madison County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Confederate and Union service records, loyalty oaths, provost marshal records, pension applications, and guerrilla warfare documentation for Madison area. Missouri's border state status means both Union and Confederate records exist.

source_profiles.source_name: Lead Mining District Land and Property Records -- Madison County
source_profiles.repository: Missouri Mines State Historic Site / Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mining claims, land patents, mining company property records, and associated community records for Madison County's lead mining operations. Missouri's Lead Belt was the nation's primary lead producer from colonial times.

source_profiles.source_name: Madison County Tax Records -- Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists and personal property assessments from 1818. List heads of household, property, livestock, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Useful as census substitutes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Generally well preserved at Missouri State Archives

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1855 from Gasconade, Osage, Pulaski. County seat: Vienna. Missouri German Belt — significant German immigrant settlement from the 1830s-1860s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Vienna holds land records from 1855. Maries County was named for the Maries River (from French 'Marie'). Strong German settlement — Westphalia was founded by German Catholic immigrants in 1835 (before the county was formed). St. Joseph Catholic Church in Westphalia has baptism and marriage records from the 1830s. Maries County Historical Society has family files. Check Gasconade, Osage, and Pulaski counties for pre-1855 records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri wine country heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1855

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1855 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation (1855)

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, administrations, and guardianship records from 1855. Missouri probate courts also handle some adoption records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records from 1855. Missouri marriage records kept at county level by Recorder of Deeds. FamilySearch has indexed many Missouri marriages.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1855-1950 for Maries. Missouri territorial census 1820; state census 1844, 1852, 1856, 1860, 1864, 1868, 1876 (incomplete). Slave schedules 1850, 1860.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Maries County. Missouri is a public land state — BLM GLO records document first transfer of land from federal government. Some areas have earlier French/Spanish colonial grants.

source_profiles.source_name: German Church Records -- Maries County
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / Concordia Historical Institute / Missouri State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Evangelical, Lutheran, Catholic, and Reformed church records for Maries. Baptisms, marriages, burials, and confirmations. Many records in German language. Concordia Historical Institute (Lutheran Church-Missouri Synod) in St. Louis holds synod records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1826 from Ralls. County seat: Palmyra.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Palmyra holds land records from 1826. Marion County is home to Hannibal, birthplace of Mark Twain (Samuel Clemens). Mark Twain Boyhood Home and Museum has local history collections. Hannibal-LaGrange University has regional archives. Marion County Historical Society maintains genealogical files. Mississippi River commerce records — Hannibal was a major river port. Significant African American records — Twain's depictions reflected real enslaved persons. Strong Southern settlement from Kentucky and Virginia. Palmyra Massacre (1862) records at Missouri State Archives. FamilySearch microfilm is extensive.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1826

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1849 from Newton. County seat: Pineville. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Pineville holds land records from 1849. McDonald County is in the extreme southwest corner of Missouri, bordering Arkansas and Oklahoma. McDonald County Historical Society has limited genealogical files. Cherokee and Osage territory — check NARA-KC for Indian agency records. Elk River valley settlement. Strong Southern Appalachian settlement heritage. Check Newton County for pre-1849 records. Lead and zinc mining in the Tri-State district. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Very remote county — church records are essential supplements.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1849

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Grundy. County seat: Princeton.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Princeton holds land records from 1845. Mercer County is in north-central Missouri near the Iowa border. Mercer County Historical Society has genealogical files. Small agricultural county — church records (Baptist, Christian, Methodist) are essential supplements. Check Grundy County for pre-1845 records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. BLM GLO records document original land entries. Princeton served as a small market town.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1837 from Cole, Pulaski. County seat: Tuscumbia.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Tuscumbia holds land records from 1837. Miller County is on the Osage River and Lake of the Ozarks. Miller County Historical Society in Tuscumbia has genealogical files. Tuscumbia is one of the smallest county seats in Missouri. Bagnell Dam (1931) creation of Lake of the Ozarks flooded communities — check Corps of Engineers relocation records. Check Cole and Pulaski counties for pre-1837 records. Ozark heritage with Southern settlement patterns. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1837

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Scott. County seat: Charleston. Missouri Bootheel (southeastern lowlands) — cotton agriculture region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Charleston holds land records from 1845. Mississippi County is in the Bootheel region with significant cotton agriculture. Mississippi County Historical Society has genealogical files. Charleston was an important river town. Significant African American population — check Freedmen's Bureau records at NARA. Sharecropping and tenant farming records are important for this region. New Madrid earthquake (1811-1812) created new land formations. Check Scott County for pre-1845 records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Strong Southern settlement from Tennessee and Mississippi.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1845

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Cole, Morgan. County seat: California.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in California holds land records from 1845. Moniteau County Historical Society in California (Missouri) has genealogical files. The county name derives from a Native American word meaning 'Great Spirit.' Check Cole and Morgan counties for pre-1845 records. Strong German settlement — Moniteau County had significant German immigrant farming communities. California (MO) was named by settlers heading to the Gold Rush. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1831 from Ralls. County seat: Paris. Part of Missouri's 'Little Dixie' — strong Southern heritage and slaveholding region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Paris holds land records from 1831. Monroe County Historical Society in Paris has genealogical files. Mark Twain Lake (Clarence Cannon Dam) area. Strong Southern settlement from Kentucky and Virginia — 'Little Dixie' region with significant enslaved persons records in probate files. Missouri's prominent slaveholding region. Check Ralls County for pre-1831 records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Paris was named after Paris, Kentucky.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1831

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1818 from St. Charles. County seat: Montgomery City.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Montgomery City holds land records from 1818. Montgomery County is in east-central Missouri along the Missouri River corridor. Montgomery County Historical Society has genealogical files. Daniel Boone lived and died in what is now Montgomery County (the Judgment Tree near Marthasville). Strong Virginia settlement — many early settlers from Boone's Kentucky companions. Check St. Charles County for pre-1818 records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Graham Cave State Park has archaeological significance but nearby pioneer-era settlement records are useful.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1818
